items.title: Publication 17 (2025): Your Federal Income Tax
items.summary: Comprehensive tax guide for individuals covering filing requirements, income, deductions, and credits for tax year 2025. Includes 2025 standard deduction amounts, new deductions under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill, and filing due date of April 15, 2026.

items.title: Publication 15 (2026): Employer's Tax Guide (Circular E)
items.summary: Employer's guide covering payroll taxes, federal income tax withholding, depositing taxes, reporting, and filing requirements for 2026.

items.title: Publication 334 (2025): Tax Guide for Small Business (Schedule C)
items.summary: Tax guide for sole proprietors and individuals who file Schedule C. Covers business income, deductible expenses, depreciation, and recordkeeping for tax year 2025.

items.title: Publication 463 (2025): Travel, Gift, and Car Expenses
items.summary: Covers deductibility of business travel, meals, entertainment, gifts, and vehicle expenses. Includes 2025 standard mileage rate of 70 cents per mile for business.

items.title: Publication 505 (2026): Tax Withholding and Estimated Tax
items.summary: Explains income tax withholding for employees and estimated tax payment requirements for self-employed individuals and others with income not subject to withholding.

items.title: Publication 527 (2025): Residential Rental Property
items.summary: Tax rules for residential rental property owners including depreciation, rental income and expenses, vacation home rules, and passive activity limitations.

items.title: Publication 541: Partnerships
items.summary: Guide covering partnership formation, operations, partner allocations, basis, distributions, and termination for federal tax purposes.

items.title: Publication 544: Sales and Other Dispositions of Assets
items.summary: Explains how to figure gain or loss on sales and dispositions of property, including capital asset rules, installment sales, and like-kind exchanges.

items.title: Publication 550: Investment Income and Expenses
items.summary: Covers taxable and nontaxable investment income, capital gains and losses, wash sale rules, and reporting requirements for investors.

items.title: Publication 559: Survivors, Executors, and Administrators
items.summary: Tax information for the survivors, executors, and administrators of decedents' estates, including income in respect of a decedent and estate tax basics.

items.title: Publication 590-A (2025): Contributions to Individual Retirement Arrangements (IRAs)
items.summary: Covers IRA contribution limits, deductibility rules, rollover rules, and the rules for traditional and Roth IRAs including the 2025 contribution limits.

items.title: Publication 590-B (2025): Distributions from Individual Retirement Arrangements (IRAs)
items.summary: Explains IRA distribution rules including required minimum distributions, early withdrawal penalties, rollovers, and special situations.

items.title: Publication 946: How to Depreciate Property
items.summary: Explains MACRS depreciation methods, Section 179 expensing election, bonus depreciation (100% under OBBB), and listed property rules.

items.title: Publication 4557: Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
items.summary: IRS guide for tax professionals on data security best practices, mandatory written information security plans (WISP), and protecting client information.

items.title: Publication 1345: Handbook for Authorized IRS e-file Providers
items.summary: Official handbook for Electronic Return Originators (EROs) and transmitters covering e-file requirements, procedures, and responsibilities.

items.title: Circular 230 (Rev. 6-2014): Regulations Governing Practice Before the IRS
items.summary: Treasury regulations (31 CFR Part 10) governing who may practice before the IRS, standards of conduct, and sanctions. Required reading for Enrolled Agents, CPAs, and attorneys.

items.title: Form 1040 & 1040-SR Instructions (2025)
items.summary: Official line-by-line instructions for filing Form 1040 and 1040-SR for tax year 2025, including all new provisions under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill.

items.title: Form 1120 Instructions: U.S. Corporation Income Tax Return
items.summary: Instructions for C corporations filing Form 1120 including income, deductions, tax computation, and schedule completion.

items.title: Form 1120-S Instructions: U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation
items.summary: Instructions for S corporations filing Form 1120-S, including Schedule K-1 preparation, shareholder basis tracking, and AAA account.

items.title: Form 1065 Instructions: U.S. Return of Partnership Income
items.summary: Instructions for partnerships and multi-member LLCs filing Form 1065, including Schedule K-1 allocations and BBA audit rules.

items.title: Form 2848: Power of Attorney and Declaration of Representative
items.summary: Authorizes a qualified representative (Enrolled Agent, CPA, attorney) to act on a taxpayer's behalf before the IRS for specified tax matters.

items.title: Form 8821: Tax Information Authorization
items.summary: Authorizes a designee to inspect or receive confidential tax information for specified tax periods and matters without granting full representative authority.

items.title: Form W-9: Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification
items.summary: Used to provide a taxpayer's TIN (SSN or EIN) to a requester for information return reporting on payments such as 1099-NEC, 1099-MISC, and 1099-INT.

items.title: Form W-4 (2026): Employee's Withholding Certificate
items.summary: Used by employees to tell their employer how much federal income tax to withhold. Updated for 2026 to reflect OBBB provisions.

items.title: IR-2026-49: Treasury, IRS Issue Final Regulations on 'No Tax on Tips' Provision
items.summary: Final regulations listing occupations where workers customarily and regularly receive tips under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill. April 10, 2026.

items.title: IR-2026-46: IRS Expands Business Tax Account Access to Partnerships and Tax-Exempt Organizations
items.summary: The IRS expanded its Business Tax Account online platform to partnerships, government entities, and tax-exempt organizations. April 6, 2026.

items.title: IR-2026-30: IRS Dirty Dozen Tax Scams for 2026
items.summary: Annual list of the top 12 tax scams threatening taxpayers, businesses, and tax professionals in 2026, including phishing, fake charities, and employee retention credit fraud.

items.title: IR-2025-103: IRS Releases Tax Inflation Adjustments for Tax Year 2026
items.summary: Annual inflation adjustments for 2026 tax year including standard deduction ($32,200 MFJ), EITC, AMT, estate tax exclusion ($15M), and other key figures under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill. October 9, 2025.

items.title: IR-2026-12: IRS Opens 2026 Filing Season
items.summary: IRS officially opened the 2026 filing season on January 26, 2026, accepting and processing federal income tax returns for tax year 2025. January 26, 2026.

items.title: IR-2026-06: IRS Issues Guidance on 100% Bonus Depreciation Under OBBB
items.summary: Notice 2026-11 provides guidance on the permanent 100% additional first year depreciation deduction for eligible property acquired after January 19, 2025. January 14, 2026.

items.title: TIR 26-1: Individual Mandate Penalties for Tax Year 2026
items.summary: Massachusetts DOR announces the penalty schedule for individuals who fail to comply with the Massachusetts Health Care Reform Act for tax year 2026.

items.title: TIR 25-9: Massachusetts Exclusion Amounts for Employer-Provided Transportation Benefits for Taxable Years Beginning in 2026
items.summary: Announces the Massachusetts exclusion amounts for employer-provided parking, transit pass, commuter highway vehicle, and bicycle commuting benefits for 2026.

items.title: TIR 25-8: Interest Rate on Overpayments and Underpayments (2026 Q1)
items.summary: Announces the quarterly interest rates on overpayments (6%) and underpayments (8%) for 2026 Q1 and confirms 6%/8% rates for all of 2025.

items.title: TIR 25-7: Annual Update of Real Estate Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker) for Tax Year 2025
items.summary: Updates income thresholds ($75,000 single, $112,000 MFJ) and maximum credit amount ($2,820) for the Senior Circuit Breaker Credit for tax year 2025.

items.title: TIR 25-5: Tax Provisions in Certain Massachusetts Legislation Enacted in 2024
items.summary: Explains new tax provisions including the Climatetech Tax Incentive Program, live theater credit, qualified internship credit, and changes to business credits from 2024 Massachusetts legislation.

items.title: TIR 25-3: State Street Corporation v. Commissioner — Financial Institution Research Tax Credit
items.summary: Addresses eligibility of financial institutions to claim the Massachusetts Research Tax Credit following the Supreme Judicial Court decision in State Street Corporation v. Commissioner of Revenue.

items.title: TIR 25-1: Individual Mandate Penalties for Tax Year 2025
items.summary: Announces penalty schedule for individuals failing to maintain qualifying health insurance in 2025 under the Massachusetts Health Care Reform Act. Penalties apply when income exceeds 150% of the Federal Poverty Level.

items.title: TIR 24-16: Tax Credit Provisions in the Affordable Homes Act
items.summary: Explains new and expanded tax credit provisions in the 2024 Affordable Homes Act affecting personal income tax and corporate excise.

items.title: TIR 24-14: Tax Provisions in the Fiscal Year 2025 Budget
items.summary: Explains FY2025 Budget law changes including IRC conformity update to January 1, 2024, repeal of bank interest deduction, Title 5 septic credit clarification, joint filing requirement exemption, and FY2025 Tax Amnesty Program.

items.title: TIR 24-4: Provisions in the 2023 Tax Relief Legislation
items.summary: Explains significant 2023 Massachusetts tax relief provisions including reduced short-term capital gains rate (8.5%), increased estate tax threshold ($2M), and expanded dependent care credit.

items.title: Directive 25-1: Taxation of Sale or Rental of Cable Television Converter Boxes and Similar Devices
items.summary: Clarifies Massachusetts sales and use tax treatment of cable television converter boxes, set-top boxes, and similar devices, including devices with additional functionality such as on-demand video and app access.

items.title: 2025 Form 1 Instructions: Massachusetts Resident Income Tax Return
items.summary: Official line-by-line instructions for Massachusetts residents filing Form 1 for tax year 2025, including new 4% surtax on income over $1 million, health care mandate Schedule HC, and all credits.

items.title: 2025 Form 1: Massachusetts Resident Income Tax Return
items.summary: Massachusetts income tax return form for full-year residents for tax year 2025.

items.title: 2025 Form 1-NR/PY: Massachusetts Nonresident/Part-Year Tax Return
items.summary: Massachusetts income tax return for nonresidents and part-year residents for tax year 2025, including allocation of Massachusetts-source income.

items.title: 2025 Schedule HC: Massachusetts Health Care Information
items.summary: Required schedule for Massachusetts residents to report health insurance coverage or exemptions for the individual mandate. Filed with Form 1 or 1-NR/PY.

items.title: 2025 Schedule B: Massachusetts Interest, Dividends and Certain Capital Gains and Losses
items.summary: Reports Massachusetts interest, dividends, short-term capital gains (taxed at 8.5%), and certain other investment income for 2025.

items.title: 2025 Schedule C: Massachusetts Profit or Loss from Business
items.summary: Massachusetts schedule for reporting profit or loss from a sole proprietorship or self-employment for tax year 2025.

items.title: 2025 Form M-4868: Massachusetts Income Tax Extension Payment Worksheet and Voucher
items.summary: Used to make a Massachusetts extension payment when at least 80% of the 2025 tax liability is paid by April 15, 2026 to avoid penalties.
